10	
11	/-!
12	# Opens and Over categories
13	
14	In this file, given a topological space `X`, and `U : Opens X`,
15	we show that the category `Over U` (whose objects are the
16	`V : Opens X` equipped with a morphism `V ⟶ U`) is equivalent
17	to the category `Opens U`.
18	
19	## TODO
20	* show that both functors of the equivalence `overEquivalence U` are continuous and
21	  induce an equivalence between `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A`
22	  and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A` for any category `A`.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	universe u
29	
30	open CategoryTheory Topology
31	
32	namespace TopologicalSpace
33	
34	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
35	
36	namespace Opens
37	
38	/-- If `X` is a topological space and `U : Opens X`,
39	then the category `Over U` is equivalent to `Opens ↥U`. -/
40	@[simps!]
41	def overEquivalence : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U where
42	  functor.obj V := ⟨_, IsOpen.preimage (continuous_subtype_val) V.left.isOpen⟩
43	  functor.map f := homOfLE (Set.preimage_mono (f := Subtype.val) (leOfHom f.left))
44	  inverse.obj W :=
45	    Over.mk (Y := ⟨_, (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 W.isOpen⟩)
46	      (homOfLE (fun _ _ ↦ by aesop))
47	  inverse.map f := Over.homMk (homOfLE (Set.image_mono (leOfHom f)))
48	  unitIso := NatIso.ofComponents (fun V ↦ Over.isoMk (eqToIso (by
49	    ext x
50	    dsimp
51	    simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_image, Set.mem_preimage,
52	      Subtype.exists, exists_and_left, exists_prop, exists_eq_right_right, iff_self_and]
53	    apply leOfHom V.hom)))
54	  counitIso := NatIso.ofComponents (fun V ↦ eqToIso (by aesop))
55	
56	end Opens
57	
58	end TopologicalSpace
59

640	
641	section OverSliceSheafEquiv
642	
643	open Topology CategoryTheory
644	
645	universe v w
646	
647	-- NOTE: within the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` namespace the unqualified
648	-- identifier `Opens` resolves to the scheme-theoretic `Scheme.Opens` (expecting a
649	-- `Scheme`), shadowing the intended point-set `TopologicalSpace.Opens`. We therefore
650	-- fully qualify every `TopologicalSpace.Opens …` here; `Over`/`Sieve`/`GrothendieckTopology`
651	-- are `CategoryTheory` names with no scheme shadow and stay unqualified.
652	
653	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
654	
655	/-- Pointwise cover-correspondence underlying `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`: a
656	sieve `S` on `W : Opens ↥U` is a covering sieve in the subspace topology iff its
657	pushforward along the open-embedding image functor `↥U ↪ X` is a covering sieve in
658	`X`. Both sides are the pointwise neighbourhood-cover condition of
659	`Opens.grothendieckTopology`, matched across the injection `Subtype.val`. -/
660	private lemma overEquiv_image_cover_iff (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥U) (S : Sieve W) :
661	    (S.functorPushforward ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U))
662	        ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
663	          (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W)
664	      ↔ S ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) W := by
665	  constructor
666	  · intro h y hy
667	    have hx : (y : X) ∈
668	        (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W) :=
669	      ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
670	    obtain ⟨V, f, hf, hxV⟩ := h y hx
671	    obtain ⟨W', a, b, hSa, _⟩ := hf
672	    refine ⟨W', a, hSa, ?_⟩
673	    have hyim : (y : X) ∈
674	        ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W' := b.le hxV
675	    obtain ⟨z, hz, hzeq⟩ := hyim
676	    rw [← (Subtype.ext hzeq : z = y)]; exact hz
677	  · intro h x hx
678	    obtain ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩ := hx
679	    obtain ⟨W', a, hSa, hyW'⟩ := h y hy
680	    refine ⟨((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W',
681	      ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).map a, ?_,
682	      ⟨y, hyW', rfl⟩⟩
683	    exact ⟨W', a, 𝟙 _, hSa, by simp⟩
684	
685	/-- The inverse of `overEquivalence U` exhibits `(Opens ↥U, subspace topology)` as a
686	dense subsite of `(Over U, slice topology)`. This is the dense-subsite datum
687	`Equivalence.sheafCongr` consumes; the only non-formal field is the pointwise
688	cover-correspondence `overEquiv_image_cover_iff`. -/
689	instance overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite :
690	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
691	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
692	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
693	  functorPushforward_mem_iff {W S} := by
694	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
695	    rw [show Sieve.overEquiv ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W)
696	          (S.functorPushforward (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)
697	        = S.functorPushforward
698	            ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U) by
699	      rw [Sieve.functorPushforward_comp]; rfl]
700	    exact overEquiv_image_cover_iff U W S
701	
702	/-- **The open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site equivalence.** For a topological space
703	`X`, an open `U : Opens X`, and any value category `A`, the slice sheaf category
704	over `U` is equivalent to the sheaf category on the open subspace `↥U`:
705	
706	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
707	
708	This completes the documented Mathlib `## TODO` at `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` and
709	is the SHARED root both ⊗-inverse bridges (`homOfLocalCompat`, `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
710	reduce to. Built by transporting `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U` across
711	`CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, using the project-local dense-subsite
712	instance `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`.
713	
714	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
715	noncomputable def overSliceSheafEquiv (A : Type w) [Category.{v} A] :
716	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌
717	      Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
718	  (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
719	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
720	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
721	
722	end OverSliceSheafEquiv
723	
724	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
32	
33	variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52	
53	section Functor
54	
55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
79	
80	end Functor
81	
82	variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}
83	
84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule
93	
94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl

246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
247	    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
248	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
250	    eqToHom (by simp) := by
251	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
252	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
258	      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
259	  simp [this]

268	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
269	  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
270	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
271	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=

280	        eqToHom (by simp) := by

282	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
283	  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
284	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
285	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=

294	a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
295	(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of

306	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
307	    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
308	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
321	  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

329	    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333

335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)

392	def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
393	  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
394	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=

396	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
397	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
398	    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

402	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
403	
404	@[simp]

406	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
407

410	    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=

412	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

416	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
417	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
418

420	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
421	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
422	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

425	def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
426	    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
427	    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
428	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
429	  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
430	      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
431	      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)
432	
433	@[simp]
434	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
435	    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
436	      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
437	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
438	  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
439	    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
440	    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _
441

444	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
445	    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
446	      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
447	  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
448	    Iso.hom_inv_id]
449	  simp

10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Whiskering

12	/-!
13	# The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme
14	
15	In this file, given a scheme `X`, we define the category of presheaves
16	of modules over `X`. As categories of presheaves of modules are
17	defined for presheaves of rings (and not presheaves of commutative rings),
18	we also introduce a definition `X.ringCatSheaf` for the underlying sheaf
19	of rings of `X`.
20	
21	-/

44	      _ _ _).obj X.ringCatSheaf where
45	  hom := Functor.whiskerRight f.c _
46	
47	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

    IsContinuous F J K :=
  isContinuous_of_preservesOneHypercovers.{max u₁ v₁} F J K

variable (A)
variable [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]

/-- The induced functor `Sheaf K A ⥤ Sheaf J A` given by `F.op ⋙ _`
if `F` is a continuous functor.
-/
@[simps!]
def sheafPushforwardContinuous : Sheaf K A ⥤ Sheaf J A :=
  ObjectProperty.lift _
    (sheafToPresheaf _ _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op)
    (F.op_comp_isSheaf J K)

/-- The functor `F.sheafPushforwardContinuous A J K : Sheaf K A ⥤ Sheaf J A`
is induced by the precomposition with `F.op`. -/
@[simps!]
def sheafPushforwardContinuousCompSheafToPresheafIso :
    F.sheafPushforwardContinuous A J K ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J A ≅
      sheafToPresheaf K A ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op := Iso.refl _

/-- The functor `sheafPushforwardContinuous` corresponding to the identity functor
identifies to the identity functor. -/
@[simps!]
def sheafPushforwardContinuousId :
    sheafPushforwardContinuous (𝟭 C) A J J ≅ 𝟭 _ := Iso.refl _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors on sheaves identifies to
the pushforward for the composition of the two functors. -/
@[simps!]
def sheafPushforwardContinuousComp [IsContinuous G K L] :
    letI := isContinuous_comp F G J K L
    sheafPushforwardContinuous G A K L ⋙ sheafPushforwardContinuous F A J K ≅
    sheafPushforwardContinuous (F ⋙ G) A J L := Iso.refl _

variable {F F'} in
/-- The action of a natural transformation on pushforward functors of sheaves. -/
@[simps]
def sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans [IsContinuous F' J K] :
    sheafPushforwardContinuous F' A J K ⟶ sheafPushforwardContinuous F A J K where

=== protected entries for SheafOverEquivalence / overEquivalence ===
=== appIso block ===
lemma isIso_app (V : Y.Opens) (hV : V ≤ f.opensRange) : IsIso (f.app V) := by
  rw [show V = f ''ᵁ f ⁻¹ᵁ V from Opens.ext (Set.image_preimage_eq_of_subset hV).symm]
  infer_instance

/-- The isomorphism `Γ(Y, f(U)) ≅ Γ(X, U)` induced by an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`. -/
def appIso (U) : Γ(Y, f ''ᵁ U) ≅ Γ(X, U) :=
  (asIso <| LocallyRingedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.invApp f.toLRSHom U).symm

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem appIso_inv_naturality {U V : X.Opens} (i : op U ⟶ op V) :
    X.presheaf.map i ≫ (f.appIso V).inv =
      (f.appIso U).inv ≫ Y.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.op.map i) :=
  PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.inv_naturality _ _

theorem appIso_hom (U) :
    (f.appIso U).hom = f.app (f ''ᵁ U) ≫ X.presheaf.map
      (eqToHom (preimage_image_eq f U).symm).op :=
  (PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.inv_invApp f.toPshHom U).trans (by rw [eqToHom_op]; rfl)

/-- A variant of `appIso_hom` that uses `Hom.appLE`. -/
theorem appIso_hom' (U) :
    (f.appIso U).hom = f.appLE (f ''ᵁ U) U (preimage_image_eq f U).ge :=
  f.appIso_hom U

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem app_appIso_inv (U) :
    f.app U ≫ (f.appIso (f ⁻¹ᵁ U)).inv =
      Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE (Set.image_preimage_subset f U.1)).op :=
  PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.app_invApp _ _

/-- A variant of `app_invApp` that gives an `eqToHom` instead of `homOfLE`. -/
@[reassoc]
theorem app_invApp' (U) (hU : U ≤ f.opensRange) :
    f.app U ≫ (f.appIso (f ⁻¹ᵁ U)).inv =
      Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom (Opens.ext <| by simpa [Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range])).op :=
  PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.app_invApp _ _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc (attr := simp), elementwise nosimp]
theorem appIso_inv_app (U) :
    (f.appIso U).inv ≫ f.app (f ''ᵁ U) = X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (preimage_image_eq f U)).op :=
  (PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.invApp_app _ _).trans (by rw [eqToHom_op])

@[reassoc (attr := simp), elementwise nosimp]
lemma appLE_appIso_inv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U : Y.Opens}
    {V : X.Opens} (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    f.appLE U V e ≫ (f.appIso V).inv =
        Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE <| (f.image_mono e).trans
          (f.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf U ▸ inf_le_right)).op := by
  simp only [appLE, Category.assoc, appIso_inv_naturality, Functor.op_obj, Functor.op_map,
    Quiver.Hom.unop_op, opensFunctor_map_homOfLE, app_appIso_inv_assoc, Opens.carrier_eq_coe]
  rw [← Functor.map_comp]
  rfl

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma appIso_inv_appLE {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V : X.Opens}
    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ f ''ᵁ U) :
    (f.appIso U).inv ≫ f.appLE (f ''ᵁ U) V e =
        X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (by rwa [preimage_image_eq] at e)).op := by
  simp only [appLE, appIso_inv_app_assoc, eqToHom_op]
  rw [← Functor.map_comp]
  rfl

lemma appIso_inv_app_presheafMap (U : X.Opens) :
    (f.appIso U).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
  rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma id_appIso (U : X.Opens) :
    (𝟙 X :).appIso U = X.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (by simp)).op := by
  ext; simp [appIso_hom]

@[simp]
lemma comp_appIso {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (U : X.Opens) :
    (f ≫ g).appIso U =
      Z.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (by simp)).op ≪≫ g.appIso _ ≪≫ f.appIso U := by
  ext : 1; simp [appIso_hom, app_eq_appLE, appLE_comp_appLE, -comp_appLE]

end Scheme.Hom

variable [G.IsDenseSubsite J K]

include J K

lemma isCoverDense : G.IsCoverDense K := isCoverDense' J
lemma isLocallyFull : G.IsLocallyFull K := isLocallyFull' J
lemma isLocallyFaithful : G.IsLocallyFaithful K := isLocallyFaithful' J

lemma coverPreserving : CoverPreserving J K G :=
  ⟨functorPushforward_mem_iff.mpr⟩

instance (priority := 900) : G.IsContinuous J K :=
  letI := IsDenseSubsite.isCoverDense J K G
  letI := IsDenseSubsite.isLocallyFull J K G
  letI := IsDenseSubsite.isLocallyFaithful J K G
  IsCoverDense.isContinuous J K G (IsDenseSubsite.coverPreserving J K G)

instance (priority := 900) : G.IsCocontinuous J K where
  cover_lift hS :=
    letI := IsDenseSubsite.isCoverDense J K G

  map_surjective α := ⟨⟨sheafHom α.hom⟩, Sheaf.hom_ext <| sheafHom_restrict_eq α.hom⟩

instance faithful_sheafPushforwardContinuous [G.IsContinuous J K] :
    Faithful (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous A J K) where
  map_injective := by
    intro ℱ ℱ' α β e
    ext1
    apply_fun fun e => e.hom at e
    dsimp [sheafPushforwardContinuous] at e
    rw [← sheafHom_eq G α.hom, ← sheafHom_eq G β.hom, e]

end IsCoverDense

/-- If `G : C ⥤ D` is cover dense and full, then the
map `(P ⟶ Q) → (G.op ⋙ P ⟶ G.op ⋙ Q)` is bijective when `Q` is a sheaf. -/
lemma whiskerLeft_obj_map_bijective_of_isCoverDense (G : C ⥤ D)
    [G.IsCoverDense K] [G.IsLocallyFull K] {A : Type*} [Category* A]
    (P Q : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ A) (hQ : Presheaf.IsSheaf K Q) :
    Function.Bijective (((whiskeringLeft Cᵒᵖ Dᵒᵖ A).obj G.op).map : (P ⟶ Q) → _) :=
  (IsCoverDense.restrictHomEquivHom (ℱ' := ⟨Q, hQ⟩)).symm.bijective

variable (G : C ⥤ D) {A : Type*} [Category* A]

/-- The functor `G : C ⥤ D` exhibits `(C, J)` as a dense subsite of `(D, K)`
if `G` is cover-dense, locally fully-faithful,
and `S` is a cover of `C` if and only if the image of `S` in `D` is a cover. -/
class IsDenseSubsite : Prop where
  isCoverDense' : G.IsCoverDense K := by infer_instance
  isLocallyFull' : G.IsLocallyFull K := by infer_instance
  isLocallyFaithful' : G.IsLocallyFaithful K := by infer_instance
  functorPushforward_mem_iff : ∀ {X : C} {S : Sieve X}, S.functorPushforward G ∈ K _ ↔ S ∈ J _

lemma functorPushforward_mem_iff {X : C} {S : Sieve X} [G.IsDenseSubsite J K] :
    S.functorPushforward G ∈ K _ ↔ S ∈ J _ := IsDenseSubsite.functorPushforward_mem_iff

namespace IsDenseSubsite

variable [G.IsDenseSubsite J K]

include J K

lemma isCoverDense : G.IsCoverDense K := isCoverDense' J
lemma isLocallyFull : G.IsLocallyFull K := isLocallyFull' J
lemma isLocallyFaithful : G.IsLocallyFaithful K := isLocallyFaithful' J

lemma coverPreserving : CoverPreserving J K G :=
  ⟨functorPushforward_mem_iff.mpr⟩

instance (priority := 900) : G.IsContinuous J K :=
  letI := IsDenseSubsite.isCoverDense J K G

/- Continuity of both legs of `Opens.overEquivalence U`.

   The site of `(↑U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf` is `Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(↑U : Scheme)`,
   where `↥(↑U : Scheme)` is the carrier of the open subscheme reached via the
   `Scheme → TopCat → Type` coercion. This is *definitionally* the subtype `↥U`, but it
   keys differently in the instance discrimination tree, so typeclass search does not find
   the project's dense-subsite instance `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite` (stated for the
   bare topological space `↥U`). We therefore state the two continuity instances on the
   scheme-carrier form and discharge them by `show`-converting to the subtype form, where
   `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite` + the priority-900 `IsDenseSubsite → IsContinuous` and
   the equivalence's functor-density propagation resolve them. -/

/-- Continuity of the inverse leg of `overEquivalence U`. -/
instance overEquivInverseIsContinuous :
    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(↑U : Scheme)) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) := by
  show (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U)
  infer_instance

/-- Continuity of the functor leg of `overEquivalence U`. -/
instance overEquivFunctorIsContinuous :
    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(↑U : Scheme)) := by
  show (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
  infer_instance

input.content:
# Picard/SheafOverEquivalence.lean

## Summary

Sorry count: **4 → 2**. Closed the LINCHPIN `overEquivalence` (axiom-clean) and `chartOverIso`
(was already a one-liner; now its `restrictOverIso`/`unitOverIso` deps are scaffolded). Built 4 new
helper decls. Remaining open: `restrictOverIso` (full body) and the single leaf inside `unitOverIso`.

- **CLOSED axiom-clean:** `overEquivalence` (the genuine content — `def:sheafofmodules_over_equivalence`).
  Verified axioms = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` only (no project/sorry axioms).
- **New helper decls (all compile, axiom-clean):**
  - `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite'`, `overEquivInverseIsContinuous`, `overEquivFunctorIsContinuous`
    — the continuity instances `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` needs.
  - `image_overEquiv_functor_obj`, `left_overEquiv_inverse_obj` — the open-equalities `ι ''ᵁ (e V) = V.left`.
  - `phiOver`, `psiOver` — the ring morphism φ and its inverse ψ (the real content), with full
    naturality proofs.
- **Still open (typed sorry, genuine partials):**
  - `restrictOverIso` (line ~233): `by exact sorry`, detailed pushforwardComp strategy in the comment.
  - `unitOverIso` (line ~249): construction COMPLETE (`(asIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit (phiOver U))).symm`),
    `IsIso (phiOver U)` PROVEN, reflection chain for `IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit (phiOver U))` done
    down to ONE leaf `sorry` (a sectionwise `IsIso` of the additive map underlying `(phiOver U).hom.app W`).

## overEquivalence (def:sheafofmodules_over_equivalence) — CLOSED axiom-clean

### Construction
`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Opens.overEquivalence U) (phiOver U) (psiOver U) H₁ H₂`.

### Key obstacles solved (REUSABLE recipes)

1. **Continuity instances did NOT auto-resolve — `↥↑U` vs `↥U` discrimination-tree mismatch.**
   The site of `(↑U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf` is `Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(↑U : Scheme)` where the
   carrier is reached via `Scheme → TopCat → Type`; this is **definitionally** the subtype `↥U` but
   **keys differently** in the instance discrimination tree, so the project's
   `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite` (stated for the bare topological space `↥U`) is NOT found by
   typeclass search, AND the priority-900 `IsDenseSubsite → IsContinuous` chain silently fails to fire.
   **FIX:** state the continuity instances on the scheme-carrier form and discharge by
   `change …(Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)…; infer_instance` (defeq-convert to the subtype form
   where the project instance + Mathlib chain resolve). `exact overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite U`
   typechecks by defeq even though `infer_instance` fails.

2. **φ / ψ construction.** Sectionwise the two structure-sheaf presheaves agree definitionally via
   `Scheme.Opens.toScheme_presheaf_obj` (`Γ(↑U, W) = Γ(X, ι ''ᵁ W)`, rfl). So
   `phiOver.hom.app V := X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (eqToHom (image_overEquiv_functor_obj U V.unop)).op`
   (and ψ symmetric with `left_overEquiv_inverse_obj`). The codomain defeq is accepted directly.

3. **Naturality / H₁ / H₂ — the `Functor.map_comp` matching wall.** `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` (and
   even the functor-pinned `rw [← F.map_comp]`) **refuses to combine** `F.map a ≫ F.map b` for
   `F = X.ringCatSheaf.obj` (a `forget₂`-composite functor) — "did not find pattern". Recipes that WORK:
   - For φ naturality: `simp only [Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous_obj_obj_map]; erw [← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]; congr 1` (erw is defeq-tolerant; works when the X.ringCatSheaf leg is FIRST in each combine).
   - For ψ naturality / H₁ / H₂ (where the `(↑U).ringCatSheaf` leg is first, causing erw to overreach
     into `opensFunctor` and spawn a metavar): **peel `forget₂` first**
     `change (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map _ ≫ … = …; rw [← (forget₂ _ _).map_comp, …]; congr 1`,
     then combine the inner `(sheafToPresheaf …).obj X.sheaf`-functor maps with `Functor.map_comp`
     applied as a **TERM** (not rw): `(Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans ((congrArg _ (Subsingleton.elim _ _)).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. Thinness of `(Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ` closes via `Subsingleton.elim`.
   - **`.val` vs `.obj` alias:** writing `X.ringCatSheaf.val.map` records as `Sheaf.val` which is
     syntactically distinct from the `.obj` that `simp` produces, blocking combines. Use
     `X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map` UNIFORMLY in every app.

## IsIso (phiOver U) — PROVEN (inside unitOverIso)
`apply isIso_of_reflects_iso (phiOver U) (sheafToPresheaf _ RingCat)` after establishing the presheaf
map is iso via `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app` + each component `= X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (eqToHom _).op`
is an iso (`inferInstanceAs`). Idiom: `isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso` is `rw`-able (see Sheafification.lean:163).

## unitOverIso — 1 leaf sorry
`(asIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (phiOver U))).symm`. Needs
`IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit (phiOver U))`. Reflection chain DONE:
`rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.forget _), ← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]; intro W` reduces to the leaf
`IsIso (((toPresheaf).map ((forget).map (unitToPushforwardObjUnit (phiOver U)))).app W)`. By
`unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply` the section is `(phiOver U).hom.app W` (a ring iso since
`hφ`); the leaf is the additive map underlying it. **NEXT:** connect the leaf to
`(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map ((phiOver U).hom.app W)` (or extract `IsIso ((phiOver U).hom.app W)`
from `hφ` via NatTrans component-iso) — a ~5-10 line finish.

## restrictOverIso — open (documented)
`(overEquivalence U).functor.obj (M.restrict U.ι) ≅ M.over U`. Sectionwise both `= Γ(M, V.left)`.
Tested `Iso.refl`/`eqToIso rfl`/`preimageIso (isoMk Iso.refl)` — NOT directly defeq at the iso level
(the maps differ by the `image_overEquiv` eqToHom). Intended route (analogist step 5): `M.restrict U.ι`
is itself a `pushforward` along `U.ι.opensFunctor`; compose with `pushforward (phiOver U)` via
`SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp` (= `Iso.refl`), then `pushforwardNatIso` along the `eqToIso` of the
two `Over U ⥤ Opens X` index functors (`Over.forget U` vs `U.ι.opensFunctor ⋙ e.functor`, both `V ↦ V.left`),
mirroring `restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso`. ~30-60 LOC of the same eqToHom/thinness machinery.

## Why I stopped
`Real progress`: closed `overEquivalence` (axiom-clean — the genuine ring-morphism content + full
equivalence: φ, ψ, H₁, H₂), the 3 continuity instances, 2 image-equality lemmas, `IsIso (phiOver U)`,
and the `unitOverIso` construction + reflection chain (1 leaf sorry). Sorry count 4 → 2.
The two remaining sorries are NOT blocked by missing Mathlib — both are mechanical finishes of the
same eqToHom/thinness/reflection machinery already developed here; I ran out of budget after the
(large) fight to get `overEquivalence` itself axiom-clean (the `↥↑U` carrier + `Functor.map_comp`
matching walls each took many iterations to crack). `chartOverIso` is the intended one-liner and
compiles (sorry-transitive only through `restrictOverIso`).

Blueprint: `overEquivalence` ready for `\leanok` (statement+proof). `chartOverIso` statement formalized.
`restrictOverIso`/`unitOverIso` statements formalized (bodies have sorry) — leave unmarked until closed.

AND the dual `sliceDualTransport` (DualInverse) as NEXT-iter consumers.

exactly as ana258-overeq predicted. Decls: `overEquivalence` (axioms = propext/Classical.choice/
Quot.sound only), `phiOver`/`psiOver` (ring morphisms φ/ψ, full naturality), `image_overEquiv_functor_obj`/
`left_overEquiv_inverse_obj` (`ι ''ᵁ (e V)=V.left`), 3 continuity instances, `IsIso (phiOver U)`.

**REUSABLE WALLS CRACKED (will recur for restrictOverIso / unitOverIso / sliceDualTransport):**
1. **`↥↑U` (scheme-`↑U` carrier via Scheme→TopCat→Type) ≠ `↥U` (subtype) in the discrimination
   tree** though DEFEQ. The continuity legs of `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` do NOT resolve
   (project `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite` is keyed on `↥U`; priority-900 `IsDenseSubsite→IsContinuous`
   silently won't fire). FIX: state instances on the `↥↑U` form, prove by
   `change …(Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)…; infer_instance`. `exact overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite U`
   typechecks by defeq where `infer_instance` fails.
2. **`Functor.map_comp` MATCHING WALL:** `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` (even functor-pinned
   `rw [← F.map_comp]`) REFUSES to combine `F.map a ≫ F.map b` for `F = X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
   (a `forget₂`-composite). Recipes: (a) `erw [← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]; congr 1`
   works ONLY when the X.ringCatSheaf leg is FIRST (else erw overreaches into `opensFunctor` →
   metavar `?g`). (b) when the `(↑U).ringCatSheaf` leg is first: **peel forget₂** via
   `change (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map _ ≫ … ; rw [← (forget₂ _ _).map_comp, …]; congr 1`,
   then combine inner `(sheafToPresheaf …).obj X.sheaf`-maps with `Functor.map_comp` as a **TERM**:
   `(Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans ((congrArg _ (Subsingleton.elim _ _)).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`
   (thinness of `(Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ`). (c) **ALWAYS use `X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map`, NEVER `.val.map`** —
   `.val`=`Sheaf.val` is syntactically distinct from the `.obj` that `simp` produces, blocking combines.
3. `isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso` is `rw`-able (Sheafification.lean:163): reflect SheafOfModules iso →
   PresheafOfModules → sectionwise via `← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.forget _), ← … (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.

- `unitOverIso` = `(asIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit (phiOver U))).symm`; reflection chain done, ONE
  leaf `IsIso` of the additive map underlying `(phiOver U).hom.app W` (needs `Sheaf RingCat`
  InducedCategory iso→component extraction from `IsIso (phiOver U)` + `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply` ext).
- `restrictOverIso` — pushforwardComp + pushforwardNatIso mirror of `restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso`
  (Mathlib `Modules/Sheaf.lean:335`), ~30-60 LOC same eqToHom/thinness machinery.
